questions.question: You are told about possible abuse. Which of these is NOT part of your role as a frontline care worker?
options: Recognising the signs of abuse
Reporting your concern to the right person without delay
Recording what you have seen or been told
Investigating the allegation to decide whether abuse definitely happened

questions.question: Someone you support is in immediate danger and a crime is happening in front of you. What should you do first?
options: Confront the alleged abuser about what they have done
Call 999
Wait until your next shift to mention it
Tidy the area before anyone else arrives

questions.question: Under the Care Act, which body is the lead agency for safeguarding enquiries?
options: The care provider
The CQC
The local authority
The police

questions.question: What protection does the Public Interest Disclosure Act 1998 provide?
options: It gives legal protection to workers who raise genuine concerns in the public interest
It requires local authorities to make section 42 enquiries
It regulates care services against fundamental standards
It sets out the five principles of mental capacity

questions.question: According to the Mental Capacity Act, capacity is described as being decision-specific and also:
options: time-specific
the same for every decision
permanent once it has been lost
something only a doctor can judge

questions.question: Under the Prevent duty, radicalisation is treated as a form of:
options: neglect
organisational abuse
exploitation
self-neglect

questions.question: What is the main role of a Safeguarding Adults Board?
options: To regulate the quality and safety of individual care services
To investigate crimes committed against adults
To bring partners together to agree how agencies protect adults locally and to learn from serious cases
To provide day-to-day personal care to adults at risk

questions.question: In multi-agency safeguarding, which partner investigates where a crime may have been committed?
options: The CQC
The care provider
The Safeguarding Adults Board
The police

questions.question: What is the CQC's role in safeguarding adults?
options: It makes best-interests decisions for people who lack capacity
It regulates care services and acts on concerns about the quality and safety of care
It gives legal protection to whistleblowers
It leads adult safeguarding and decides whether a formal enquiry is needed

questions.question: A person you support has no identity documents, seems fearful of a controlling companion, works excessive hours and keeps none of their own earnings. Which category of abuse do these signs most suggest?
options: Organisational abuse
Self-neglect
Modern slavery
Discriminatory abuse

questions.question: Discriminatory abuse involves harassment or unequal treatment based on:
options: a person's level of care and support needs
a person's lack of mental capacity
the routines and culture of a service
a protected characteristic such as race, religion, disability, age or sex

questions.question: Under the Mental Capacity Act, anything done for a person who lacks capacity must be:
options: in their best interests
kept secret from the person
the cheapest option available
decided solely by their family

questions.question: When someone begins to disclose abuse to you, the best response is to:
options: go and challenge the person they are describing
promise them it will stay between the two of you
stay calm, give them your full attention and let them speak in their own words
ask lots of detailed questions to get the full story quickly

questions.question: Which of the following best describes self-neglect?
options: Abuse carried out by a paid carer
Financial exploitation of an adult by a relative
Where an adult neglects their own health, hygiene or surroundings, or hoards to a degree that puts them at risk
Neglect caused by a service's routines and culture

questions.question: You should never promise to keep a disclosure of abuse secret, because you have a duty to pass concerns on.
options: True
False

questions.question: If someone is in immediate danger or a serious crime is taking place, you should call 999 first.
options: True
False

questions.question: Because safeguarding respects a person's wishes, you should never report a concern if an adult with capacity asks you not to.
options: True
False

questions.question: When there may have been a crime, it is helpful to wash, tidy and move things in the area before reporting the concern.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of the following should you NOT do when someone discloses abuse to you? (Select all that apply)
options: Reassure the person that they were right to tell you
Ask leading questions or press the person for detail
Promise to keep the disclosure secret
Confront the alleged abuser

questions.question: In which situations should you still report a safeguarding concern even if the person does not want you to? (Select all that apply)
options: Where others may be at risk
Where a crime may have been committed
Where the person may be under coercion
Where the person simply prefers you not to make a fuss

questions.question: Which of the following are recognised warning signs that an adult may be experiencing abuse? (Select all that apply)
options: A consistently well-explained set of injuries
Sudden changes in mood or withdrawal
Fearfulness around certain people
Reluctance to be left alone with someone

questions.question: Which of the following are partners that work together in multi-agency safeguarding, each with a distinct role? (Select all that apply)
options: The alleged abuser's family
The Care Quality Commission
The police
The Public Interest Disclosure Act 1998
The local authority

questions.question: If someone is in immediate danger or a serious crime is taking place, you should call ___ first.
You should never ___ to keep a disclosure of abuse secret, because you have a duty to pass the concern on.
Radicalisation is treated as a form of ___, where a vulnerable person is drawn towards extremism or terrorism by others.
